---
title: Plugins
calepin:
  plugins:
    - txtfmt
---

Plugins are `.wasm` files that extend *Calepin* with custom span/div filters, shortcodes, and postprocessors. A single `.wasm` file works on macOS, Linux, and Windows---no recompilation needed.


# Using a plugin

1. Copy the `.wasm` file to `_calepin/plugins/` (or `~/.config/calepin/plugins/`).
2. List it in front matter:

```yaml
calepin:
  plugins:
    - txtfmt
    - lightbox
    - lorem
```

Example plugins and source code: [github.com/vincentarelbundock/calepin](https://github.com/vincentarelbundock/calepin) in the `plugins/` directory.


# Available plugins

## `txtfmt` --- text formatting

The `.txtfmt` span class provides inline styling. Attributes: `em` (size multiplier), `color` (name or `#hex`), `smallcaps`, `underline`, `mark`. Over 1000 color names (from R and LaTeX) are resolved to hex for consistent rendering across formats.

[teal text]{.txtfmt color="teal"},
[large hex color]{.txtfmt color="#E69F00" em=1.3},
[highlighted]{.txtfmt mark=true},
[Small Caps]{.txtfmt smallcaps=true underline=true},
and [small orange]{.txtfmt em=0.8 color="#E69F00" underline=true}.

## `lightbox` --- click-to-enlarge images

See [Figures](figures.qmd#lightbox) for details on the lightbox plugin.

## `lorem` --- placeholder text

A shortcode plugin that generates lorem ipsum text. Useful for prototyping layouts.

- {{{< lorem >}}} --- 1 paragraph (default)
- {{{< lorem 3 >}}} --- 3 paragraphs
- {{{< lorem words=20 >}}} --- 20 words
- {{{< lorem sentences=4 >}}} --- 4 sentences
- {{{< lorem paragraphs=2 >}}} --- 2 paragraphs

## `imgur` --- image upload

The `.imgur` span uploads local images to imgur and returns format-appropriate markup. Usage: `[path/to/image.png]{.imgur alt="description"}`.

## `revealjs` --- reveal.js slides

A postprocessor that converts rendered HTML into a [reveal.js](https://revealjs.com) slide deck. Each `<h2>` heading starts a new slide.

```yaml
calepin:
  plugins:
    - revealjs
```

## `slidev` --- Slidev slides

A postprocessor that converts rendered HTML into [Slidev](https://sli.dev) markdown format.


# Writing plugins

A plugin is a Rust cdylib crate compiled to `wasm32-unknown-unknown`. It exports one or more functions that exchange JSON with *Calepin* via [extism](https://extism.org). Plugins can also be written in Go, JavaScript, Python, Zig, or any language with an extism PDK.

## Plugin functions

A plugin can export any combination of these functions:

### `filter`

Handles fenced divs and bracketed spans. Receives a JSON object:

```json
{
  "context": "div",
  "content": "<p>rendered children</p>",
  "classes": ["myclass"],
  "id": "my-id",
  "format": "html",
  "attrs": {"key": "value"}
}
```

Returns `{"Rendered": "output html"}` to handle the element, or `"Pass"` to skip.

### `shortcode`

Handles `{{< name args >}}` shortcodes. Receives:

```json
{
  "name": "lorem",
  "args": ["3"],
  "kwargs": {"words": "50"},
  "format": "html"
}
```

Returns the replacement string or `null` to skip.

### `postprocess`

Transforms the entire rendered body after all other processing. Receives:

```json
{
  "body": "<h1>...</h1><p>...</p>",
  "format": "html",
  "title": "Document Title",
  "css": "..."
}
```

Returns the modified body string.

## Minimal example

`Cargo.toml`:

```toml
[package]
name = "myplugin"
version = "0.0.1"
edition = "2021"

[lib]
crate-type = ["cdylib"]

[dependencies]
extism-pdk = "1"
serde = { version = "1", features = ["derive"] }
```

`src/lib.rs`:

```rust
use extism_pdk::*;
use serde::Deserialize;
use std::collections::HashMap;

#[derive(Deserialize)]
struct FilterContext {
    context: String,
    content: String,
    classes: Vec<String>,
    format: String,
    attrs: HashMap<String, String>,
}

#[derive(serde::Serialize)]
enum FilterResult {
    Rendered(String),
    Pass,
}

#[plugin_fn]
pub fn filter(Json(ctx): Json<FilterContext>) -> FnResult<Json<FilterResult>> {
    if ctx.context != "span" || !ctx.classes.contains(&"hello".to_string()) {
        return Ok(Json(FilterResult::Pass));
    }
    let out = match ctx.format.as_str() {
        "html" => format!("<b>{}</b>", ctx.content),
        "latex" => format!("\\textbf{{{}}}", ctx.content),
        _ => ctx.content,
    };
    Ok(Json(FilterResult::Rendered(out)))
}
```

Build with:

```bash
cargo build --release --target wasm32-unknown-unknown
```

The `.wasm` file will be at `target/wasm32-unknown-unknown/release/myplugin.wasm`.

See `plugins/txtfmt/src/lib.rs` and `plugins/lorem/src/lib.rs` in the repository for complete examples.
